diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling Genshin Impact Player Support, this is Sam. How can I help today?
[Caller] I got charged twice for the same Genesis Crystal pack. Two charges, same amount, same day. I only meant to buy one.
[Agent] Ugh, duplicate charges are frustrating and we'll make this right. Let me pull your account — UID please?
[Caller] 832118007.
[Agent] Thanks, Yuki. For verification, confirming last four digits of the payment method on file or the email address?
[Caller] Email is ytanaka at email dot com.
[Agent] Verified. Let me look at your recent transactions. I see two orders on the same day, both 99.99 USD for the Topaz Sliver pack — order IDs ending 4471 and 4472, timestamps 58 seconds apart. Classic double-submit pattern, probably a tap that didn't seem to register so you tapped again. Both charges went through and both delivered Genesis Crystals to your account.
[Caller] Yes, exactly what happened. I want a refund on the second one.
[Agent] Completely fair. Here's what I can do. I'll process a full refund on order 4472 to your original payment method — that'll appear in 5 to 7 business days. Since that pack delivered 6,480 Genesis Crystals plus 6,480 first-time bonus, I need to reverse those credits. Looking at your account... you've spent zero crystals from the second pack, still sitting in your balance, so the reversal is clean — no negative balance risk.
[Caller] Perfect, that's straightforward.
[Agent] Processing now. Refund filed, 6,480 crystals deducted from current balance, first-time bonus status preserved on the original order. You'll get a confirmation email within 10 minutes and the refund itself in 5 to 7 business days.
[Caller] Thank you, that was fast.
[Agent] You're welcome. One small tip for the future — if a top-up button seems unresponsive, wait 10 seconds before tapping again; the confirmation sometimes lags behind the charge. And you can always check the Shop history tab before re-tapping. Anything else I can help with?
[Caller] No, that was it. Really appreciate it.
[Agent] Glad we got it sorted, Yuki. Have a great day!
